items.title: Canada commits $270M to Ukraine as Carney addresses European summit in Armenia
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says his government will contribute $270 million to help Ukraine secure critical military capabilities in its defence against Russia's full-scale invasion.

items.title: Elon Musk vs OpenAI
items.summary_auto: Elon Musk is suing OpenAI and Microsoft in a trial that could dramatically reshape the AI industry.

items.title: Archives see surge in Americans requesting family records to access Canadian citizenship
items.summary_auto: The Provincial Archives of New Brunswick is sifting through a backlog of over 1,000 requests after the federal government changed the rules for citizenship by descent.

items.title: Reeling from crisis, P.E.I.'s oyster industry hopes disease-resistant seed from U.S. helps
items.summary_auto: Developed by Rutgers University in New Jersey, the seed is hoped to be resistant to MSX and dermo, the two diseases that have ravaged the province’s oyster industry.

items.title: Her sexual allegations against a sergeant were finally being investigated. Did a jealous officer trigger it?
items.summary_auto: An investigation into an Ottawa police sergeant who died in March is raising questions about what information the force had when sexual misconduct allegations made by college students surfaced four years ago and resurfaced three years ago — and about why the criminal investigation only began this year.

items.title: Dog walker bitten on the job can't sue because she 'owned' the pet at the time, Ontario's highest court rules
items.summary_auto: Ontario's highest court has ruled a part-time dog walker who was bitten on the job cannot sue her clients for damages because, under provincial law, she's considered to have been the "owner" of the animal at the time.

items.title: FIFA World Cup: Everything (and more) you need to know about the 16 stadiums
items.summary_auto: We take a closer look at the 16 World Cup stadiums for this year's tournament.

items.title: Widow of Windsor officer murdered 20 years ago says Ont. support group is a ‘lifeline’
items.summary_auto: Twenty years ago, Shelley Atkinson says she experienced “the worst day of my life” after her husband Const. John Atkinson was shot and killed while working as a police officer in Windsor. The Survivors Of Law Enforcement (SOLE) group is made up of 25 widows across Ontario who have lost a spouse in the line of duty.

items.title: This mother says Air Canada ignored refund plea for months after her child's death
items.summary_auto: A Nova Scotia mother says following her 10-year-old daughter’s death, Air Canada failed to refund one of the flights for a trip she'd planned for them, leaving her to chase down answers for months during an already devastating time. Experts say the experience highlights a deeper problem — one where customers are left without clear answers or accountability during their most vulnerable moments.

items.title: Raptors eliminated from NBA playoffs after losing Game 7 to Cavaliers 114-102
items.summary_auto: The Toronto Raptors were eliminated from the NBA playoffs on Sunday after losing to the Cavaliers in their Game 7 matchup in Cleveland.

items.title: Rudy Giuliani is hospitalized 'in critical but stable condition,' spokesperson says
items.summary_auto: Rudolph Giuliani, the ex-mayor of New York City and a former adviser to U.S. President Donald Trump, is in critical but stable condition at a hospital, his spokesperson said Sunday.

items.title: Habs score Game 7 winner while CBC reporter on air
items.summary_auto: The CBC's Kwabena Oduro was on the scene as the Bell Centre exploded in cheers following Alex Newhook's goal at the 11:07 mark of the third period in Game 7, giving the Montreal Canadiens a 2-1 lead over the Tampa Bay Lightning. The goal would prove to be the game winner.

items.title: Habs hang on in Game 7, eliminate Lightning, move on to face Buffalo Sabres
items.summary_auto: Following the Montreal Canadiens' Sunday night victory in Tampa, the Habs will now face the Buffalo Sabres. The two teams will kick off their second-round matchup on Wednesday.

items.title: Hundreds of youths descend on Vancouver's Scientology building as part of speedrunning trend
items.summary_auto: The viral trend, dubbed "Scientology speedrunning," took off in early April, with users on social media posting videos of themselves entering — or in Saturday’s case trying to enter — properties owned or inhabited in some way by the Church of Scientology.

items.title: Flooding closes Alaska Highway, cuts off access to U.S.-Canada border
items.summary_auto: The Alaska Highway is closed from Beaver Creek Canada Customs to the U.S. border as crews work to address the issue.

items.title: Maple Leafs hire Mats Sundin, former Coyotes GM John Chayka to lead front office
items.summary_auto: The Toronto Maple Leafs have landed on a new brain trust. The Original Six franchise looked both forward and back Sunday, naming ex-captain Mats Sundin as its senior executive adviser of hockey operations and John Chayka as general manager.

items.title: When oysters 'attack': B.C. team rescues heron from clingy shellfish
items.summary_auto: Animal rescue experts sprang into action this weekend to save a Pacific great blue heron that got its claw stuck in a large oyster.

items.title: Suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship kills 3, World Health Organization says
items.summary_auto: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean has killed three people and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organization and South Africa's Department of Health said on Sunday.

items.title: Sask. spring flooding washes out roads, leaving people across the province trapped or displaced
items.summary_auto: Extensive spring flooding has closed over a dozen roads across the province, displacing some and leaving around 19 people stranded near Struthers Lake Regional Park.

items.title: Agent at correspondents' dinner was hit by bullet from suspect's shotgun, prosecutors say
items.summary_auto: Authorities have determined that buckshot from the gun of the man charged with trying to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in an attempt to kill U.S. President Donald Trump struck a Secret Service agent, according to the federal prosecutor overseeing the investigation.

items.title: Maritimes brace for rain, wind — maybe snow — as storm moves into region
items.summary_auto: High winds and strong rains are expected to sweep through the Maritimes on Sunday, with snow possible in some areas.
